title: Dark World (1992) - RetroLook
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N6qK7AUqn54

Daniel (@dungeondive) marvels at Dark World, Mattel's 1992 HeroQuest rival — a game whose toy factor is simply stunning. The production is off the charts for an early-90s mass-market title: a multi-part plastic castle pegged to the board, treasure chests that open to reveal weapons, hero minis with (very stiff) hit-point clickers, removable weapons you upgrade to golden magic versions, doors that actually open, and the "Mace of Chaos" you shake to set turn order. Table presence to die for, topped by phenomenal sword-and-sorcery box art.

But — and it's a big but — once the wow factor fades, it's easily the weakest of the HeroQuest wannabes as a game. There are no scenarios, no quests, no quest book: heroes simply bust door to door through a fixed linear maze toward Korak the Cobra Nemesis Lord, competing for points via kills, coins and a hand of hit/miss cards. Combat is bare custom-dice symbol-matching; there's no exploration, no stat checks, no real item discovery. A gorgeous object and a dull game — worth picking up cheap for the spectacle alone.

----

Dark World is, for Daniel, a stunning toy wrapped around a hollow game. Do you own anything you keep purely for its table presence and spectacle, knowing the gameplay underneath barely holds up?
